title: Marsune (Gungan)

{{Quote|Mesa fight the good fight now! Mesa fight for Boss Gallo!|Marsune|<ref name="SW:GB" />}}
'''Marsune''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Otolla/Legends|Otolla]] [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] who lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3000 BBY]]. During a period of [[War of the Gungan tribes|war]] between the Gungan tribes at that [[Time/Legends|time]], Marsune formed a rogue [[Marsune's gang|gang]] and gained a reputation among the Gungans as a thief who would steal from the greedier [[Boss/Legends|Bosses]] and distribute their wealth among the poorer tribes. While spending time in his [[Tree/Legends|forest]] [[Marsune's base|base]], Marsune encountered Boss [[Gallo/Legends|Gallo]], who had escaped the [[Destruction of Otoh Sancture|destruction]] of his [[City/Legends|city]], [[Otoh Sancture]], at the hands of a [[Bursa clan (Otoh Sancture)|clan]] of [[Bursa/Legends|bursas]], a [[Sentience/Legends|semi-sentient]] carniverous species native to Naboo. Marsune, wishing to avoid being caught up in the war, agreed to only exchange four worker [[Glurrg]]s for five [[Nerf/Legends|nerfs]] Gallo had found in the forest.

Gallo went into the forest and raised a small army able to [[Battle of Otoh Sancture|attack]] the bursas that had destroyed Otoh Sancture, and upon finishing the task encountered Marsune again, who had changed his mind about the situation and wanted to help Gallo. The two Gungans then began a crusade to unite the warring tribes and defeat [[Rogoe/Legends|Rogoe]], the [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] who had secretly arranged Otoh Sancture's destruction. Marsune and Gallo traveled to each of the major Gungan cities, and completed several different tasks to secure their allegiance. Eventually Gallo prepared for an [[Raid on Spearhead|assault]] on Rogoe's city, [[Spearhead/Legends|Spearhead]], and Marsune turned the tide of the attack by recruiting the [[Bongomeken Collective]], a company that made [[Tribubble bongo/Legends|bongo]] submarines, which were necessary to attack the underwater portion of Spearhead. Rogoe was killed in the battle and Spearhead was rebuilt by Gallo as [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]], and Marsune was appointed as the first [[Captain/Legends|captain]] in Gallo's new [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|Grand Army]]. Marsune and Gallo were remembered as heroes by generations of Gungans that came after them.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Mesa thought about yousa words. Ugh—mesa have no stomach for this Rogoe. Mesa wish to see himsa fall.|Marsune to Gallo|<ref name="SW:GB" />}}
Marsune was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Otolla/Legends|Otolla]] [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] who lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]<ref name="SW:GB" /> in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3000 BBY]]<ref name="EA" /> during a long period of [[War of the Gungan tribes|war]] between the Gungan tribes. In this [[Time/Legends|time]] Marsune became a folk hero to the local Gungans, gaining a widespread reputation for stealing the riches of the greedier [[Boss/Legends|Bosses]] and distributing them amongst the poorer citizens. The Gungan also led a small [[Marsune's gang|gang]] of fellow rogue Gungans, who spent most of their time hiding in Naboo's swamps and [[Tree/Legends|forests]], and also had a [[Marsune's base|base]] hidden near the [[City/Legends|city]] [[Otoh Sancture]]. Marsune spent most of the Gungan war in a relatively isolated existence until [[Gallo/Legends|Gallo]], the Boss of Otoh Sancture, found Marsune's base following his city's [[Destruction of Otoh Sancture|destruction]] by [[Bursa/Legends|bursas]], a dangerous [[Sentience/Legends|semi-sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] on Naboo. The creatures had been sent by [[Rogoe/Legends|Rogoe]], a Gungan [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] secretly manipulating the conflict between the Gungan tribes for his own benefit, who wanted to [[Death/Legends|kill]] Gallo as he was one of the more powerful Bosses and if he decided to join could change the tide of the war. Marsune desired to stay out of the war, and so only agreed to trade five [[Nerf/Legends|nerfs]], an edible animal species, from Gallo, in exchange for four [[Glurrg]]s, a non-sentient species useful as laborers. Gallo consented to the deal and left Marsune's base with the Glurrgs, and as he explored the forest later he discovered that Rogoe was behind the destruction of his city, prompting the Boss to raise a small [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|army]] to [[Battle of Otoh Sancture|attack]] the bursas who had nested on Otoh Sancture's remains.<ref name="SW:GB" />

After Gallo was victorious in slaying the bursas Marsune went to the ruins of Otoh Sancture and agreed to join the boss's campaign against Rogoe, as the revelation of the warlord's hand in Otoh Sancture's destruction rankled Marsune's sense of honor. Marsune partnered with Gallo as he traveled through the warring Gungan cities and made [[Battle of the Forest Cities|efforts]] to unite them to his cause. With the Gungan tribes united in a [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|Grand Army]], Marsune participated in the [[Raid on Spearhead]], a major assault on Rogoe's fortress [[City/Legends|city]] [[Spearhead/Legends|Spearhead]]. The captain informed Gallo that the [[Army/Legends|army]] would be unable to take Spearhead without an underwater force, and Gallo tasked Marsune with traveling to the [[Bongomeken Collective base|base]] of the [[Bongomeken Collective]] to acquire the necessary vessels. Marsune succeeded and Rogoe and his army were ultimately destroyed, with Gallo pledging to rebuild Spearhead as [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]], a symbol of Gungan unity. The two leaders became legendary figures in Gungan history, with [[Youngling/Legends|children]] recreating their adventures in the game "[[Marsune and Gallo]]" on Naboo's forests and swamps.<ref name="SW:GB" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Oho! Marsune, Prince of Rogues! Haven yousa come to rob us then?|A Bongomeken Collective employee to Marsune|<ref name="SW:GB" />}}
Marsune had a reputation as a rogue and a thief, although he had noble intentions by redistributing the wealth to poorer Gungans. When Gallo came to Marsune for help, the captain rejected his plea to avenge Otoh Sancture, mockingly referring to it as a "grand quest" and laughing. Upon learning of Rogoe's involvement Marsune had a change of heart, becoming a steadfast supporter of Gallo and believing in the morality of their cause.<ref name="SW:GB" />

==Equipment==
Marsune rode a [[Kaadu/Legends|kaadu]] during the war between the Gungans.<ref name="SW:GB" />

==Behind the scenes==
Marsune was first mentioned in [[Prima Games]] [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]]<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> for the [[2001]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'', before appearing in the game on its release. Marsune was voiced by [[David Jeremiah]].<ref name="SW:GB" />

Marsune's tactics of robbing rich bosses, and giving to poorer tribes, are evocative of the legendary [[wikipedia:England|English]] folk hero [[wikipedia:Robin Hood|Robin Hood]].
